title: oxalis alba
intro: Oxalis alba, also known as the White Wood Sorrel or False Shamrock, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Oxalis genus and is native to Europe and Western Asia. The medicinal properties of Oxalis alba include its high content of oxalic acid, which has been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, including arthritis, gout, and skin conditions. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as an antiseptic. Oxalis alba can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and salves, which are used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and other health issues.
